Llano County Stucco Services

From lakeside retreats along the Highland Lakes to ranch homes in the rolling hill country, Llano County properties deserve stucco that holds up to the elements. Star Stucco of Austin brings over eight years of hands-on experience to every project in the area, whether you need a full exterior installation or targeted repairs on an aging finish.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Stucco Built for Llano County Living

Llano County sits at the western edge of the Texas Hill Country, where granite outcrops, open pastures, and sprawling lake shorelines define the landscape. Homes here face a unique combination of intense summer heat, sudden downpours, and expansive clay soils that shift with the seasons. Traditional stucco and stone-stucco combinations are popular across the county, but they require careful installation and maintenance to stay watertight.

Properties along Lake Buchanan and Inks Lake deal with higher humidity levels and reflected UV exposure that can accelerate surface wear. In towns like Llano and Kingsland, older homes may show hairline cracks or discoloration from decades of thermal cycling. Our team understands these local conditions and tailors every repair or installation to match the demands of the environment.

Whether you own a weekend cabin near Tow or a permanent residence in Horseshoe Bay, we apply proven techniques that protect your walls against moisture intrusion, cracking, and premature aging. We also work with stone-and-stucco facades that are common throughout the county, ensuring seamless transitions between materials.

Stucco Services We Offer in Llano County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Llano County.

Stucco Repair

Llano County homes endure wide temperature swings that cause stucco to crack and separate over time. We restore damaged sections using compatible materials so repairs blend naturally with the original finish and hold up through future heat cycles.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New construction around the Highland Lakes area benefits from a properly applied stucco exterior. We handle everything from lath and scratch coats to the final texture, building each layer to resist the moisture and UV conditions common in Llano County.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Shifting granite-rich soils in Llano County produce settlement cracks that let water behind the finish. We trace each crack to its source, stabilize the substrate, and apply flexible patching compounds that move with the structure instead of breaking apart.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Lake-area humidity and seasonal storms can drive moisture behind stucco surfaces, softening the brown coat and promoting mold growth. We remove compromised material, address drainage issues, and rebuild the wall system to prevent repeat damage.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Many Llano County homes feature traditional lime or cement plaster finishes that require periodic maintenance. We match existing plaster profiles and apply fresh coats that protect against the harsh sun while preserving the character of the original design.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS provides excellent insulation for lake homes that run air conditioning heavily during summer months. We install and repair synthetic stucco systems with proper moisture barriers, keeping energy costs down and preventing water from entering the wall cavity.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Shops, restaurants, and lodges throughout Llano and Kingsland rely on durable stucco exteriors to attract visitors. We work around business hours to minimize disruption while delivering commercial-grade finishes that stand up to heavy foot traffic areas.

Learn More

Detailed inspections that identify hidden moisture behind walls before repairs begin

We understand Llano County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Llano County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Llano County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Llano County

Llano County properties face a distinct set of challenges driven by granite-based soils, intense sun exposure, and proximity to open water. Here are the most common stucco issues we see across the area.

Cracks

The rocky, mineral-rich soil in Llano County shifts unevenly as moisture levels change, creating stress cracks in stucco walls. These range from fine hairline marks to wider structural gaps that need prompt attention to prevent water entry.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Trapped moisture from lake-area humidity can soften the base coat beneath the finish, causing visible bubbles or spongy patches. Left unchecked, these areas become entry points for deeper water damage.

Water stains

Heavy rain events followed by rapid drying leave mineral deposits and discoloration on stucco surfaces. Homes near Buchanan Dam and Inks Lake are especially prone to staining from iron-rich runoff.

Window and door leaks

Sealant around windows and doors dries out quickly under constant UV exposure in Llano County. When these seals fail, water migrates behind the stucco and damages framing and insulation.

Failing patches

Previous repair work done with mismatched materials often pulls away from the surrounding stucco within a few years. We see this frequently on older homes in Llano where quick fixes were applied without proper surface preparation.

Separation around trim

Wood and metal trim pieces expand and contract at different rates than stucco, opening gaps along edges. In the dry Hill Country heat, these separations can widen significantly during summer months.

Impact damage

Hail, wind-blown debris, and even wildlife can chip or dent stucco on rural Llano County properties. These impact marks may seem cosmetic, but they compromise the weatherproof barrier of the wall.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Years of direct sun exposure bleach pigmented stucco finishes unevenly, while algae growth on shaded north-facing walls adds dark streaks. Both issues are common across Llano County and can be corrected with proper cleaning and refinishing.

How We Work in Llano County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Contact us by phone or through our website to set up a convenient time. We travel throughout Llano County, including lake communities and rural properties, so distance is never an obstacle.

2

Review scope

Our technician examines your stucco surfaces, checks for hidden moisture with a meter, and documents every area that needs attention. We explain what we find in plain language so you understand the full picture.

3

Get estimate

You receive a detailed written estimate that breaks down materials, labor, and timeline. There are no surprise fees — the price we quote is the price you pay.

4

Complete work

Our crew arrives on schedule with all materials ready. We complete the work efficiently, clean up thoroughly, and walk you through the finished results before we leave your property.
